name: rev12-hold-cap53
signal: buffered prior open-to-open move on Coinbase 5m: |(o[t]-o[t-1])/o[t-1]| >= 12bps
side: contrarian (fade the prior move); ties resolve Up per market rule
entry: buy contrarian side early in window (live engine median 10s), effective cost (ask+1c) <= 0.53; tighter than live revEntryMax=0.55 because EV(q_TEST=0.5539) < 0 above 53c
exit: hold to resolution; timed exits at +2/+3/+4 min are fee-dead
adaptation: none; trailing-10d walk-forward threshold re-selection underperforms fixed 12bps
breakeven: q*(p) = p + 0.07*p*(1-p); q*(0.51)=0.5275, q*(0.53)=0.5474 -- NOTE q at 12bps only exceeds q*(0.51) in the recent regime
caveats: Edge is non-stationary: 10d windows q = 0.572/0.477/0.507/0.552/0.529/0.579; days 10-30 were flat-to-inverted
TRAIN (first 40d) fails the 51c fee hurdle; 'promising' verdict rests on TEST + live-book corroboration
Contrarian side is NOT reliably 51c at t0+20s: pm median 52.5c, 61% of signals above 51c; fill discipline is the binding constraint
20+bps signals show q_TEST=0.5648 (+0.0373/share at 51c) but TRAIN inverts (0.518 < 12-20bps 0.526); size gradient is TEST-only, do not upweight
